children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The agency co-chair of the interagency committee established under shall, in consultation with the Office of Science and Technology Policy, designate or establish an advisory committee on engineering biology research and development (in this section referred to as the “advisory committee”) to be composed of not fewer than 12 members, including representatives of research and academic institutions, industry, and nongovernmental entities, who are qualified to provide advice on the Initiative.section 19133 of this title

The current state of United States competitiveness in engineering biology, including the scope and scale of United States investments in engineering biology research and development in the international context.
Current market barriers to commercialization of engineering biology products, processes, and tools in the United States.
Progress made in implementing the Initiative.
The need to revise the Initiative.
The balance of activities and funding across the Initiative.
Whether the strategic plan developed or updated by the interagency committee established under is helping to maintain United States leadership in engineering biology.section 19133 of this title
Whether ethical, legal, environmental, safety, security, and other appropriate societal issues are adequately addressed by the Initiative.
The findings of the advisory committee’s assessment under subsection (b).
The advisory committee’s recommendations for ways to improve the Initiative.

children.heading: Application of Federal Advisory Committee Act
children.label: d Application of Federal Advisory Committee Act
children.text: Section 14 of the Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.) shall not apply to the advisory committee.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Termination
children.label: e Termination
children.text: The advisory committee established under subsection (a) shall terminate on the date that is 10 years after .August 9, 2022
